events.title: Spanish flu pandemic
events.desc: The H1N1 pandemic kills ~50M worldwide and cuts life expectancy by ~12 years in the worst-hit countries.

events.title: Wall Street crash — Great Depression begins
events.desc: The October 1929 crash triggers a worldwide depression; US GDP per capita falls by roughly a third by 1933.

events.title: Bretton Woods conference
events.desc: 44 nations design the post-war monetary order: dollar pegged to gold, IMF and World Bank founded.

events.title: Currency reform — the Wirtschaftswunder begins
events.desc: The Deutsche Mark replaces the Reichsmark; price controls lift and West German output takes off.

events.title: Groningen gas field discovered
events.desc: One of the world's largest gas fields transforms Dutch exports — and later coins the term 'Dutch disease'.

events.title: First Walmart opens
events.desc: Sam Walton opens the first Walmart in Rogers, Arkansas — the foundation of the Walton family's retail fortune.

events.title: Tokyo Olympics & first Shinkansen
events.desc: The 1964 Games and the bullet train showcase Japan's post-war economic miracle to the world.

events.title: Singapore becomes independent
events.desc: Separated from Malaysia, Singapore starts its rise from low-income port to one of the richest economies per capita.

events.title: Buffett takes over Berkshire Hathaway
events.desc: Warren Buffett assumes control of a struggling textile mill and turns it into his investment holding company.

events.title: Nixon shock — end of the gold standard
events.desc: The US suspends dollar convertibility into gold; the Bretton Woods system unravels and currencies float.

events.title: Bangladesh becomes independent
events.desc: Independence from Pakistan after the liberation war; from one of the poorest countries, Bangladesh begins a steady rise.

events.title: OPEC oil embargo — first oil shock
events.desc: Oil prices quadruple within months; oil-exporter incomes spike while the world economy slides into recession.

events.title: Infitah — Sadat opens the economy
events.desc: Egypt's 'open door' policy invites foreign capital and private business after decades of state control.

events.title: Microsoft founded
events.desc: Bill Gates and Paul Allen start Microsoft in Albuquerque to sell a BASIC interpreter.

events.title: First Zara store
events.desc: Amancio Ortega opens the first Zara shop in A Coruña — the seed of the Inditex fast-fashion empire.

events.title: UK goes to the IMF
events.desc: The sterling crisis forces Britain to take a $3.9B IMF loan — then the largest ever — in exchange for deep spending cuts.

events.title: Oracle founded
events.desc: Larry Ellison co-founds Software Development Laboratories — later renamed Oracle after its database.

events.title: China launches Reform and Opening
events.desc: Deng Xiaoping's market reforms begin — the start of the fastest sustained growth episode in recorded history.

events.title: Iranian revolution
events.desc: The monarchy falls; revolution, sanctions and the ensuing Iran–Iraq war reshape the economy for decades.

events.title: Thatcher era begins
events.desc: Margaret Thatcher takes office: monetarism, privatization and union curbs redirect the British economy.

events.title: WHO declares smallpox eradicated
events.desc: After the last natural case in Somalia (1977), the WHO certifies smallpox eradicated — the first disease ever eliminated by vaccination.

events.title: Bloomberg LP founded
events.desc: After leaving Salomon Brothers, Michael Bloomberg builds the terminal business that funds his fortune.

events.title: Mexico defaults — Latin American debt crisis
events.desc: Mexico's default on $80B of foreign debt opens Latin America's 'lost decade'.

events.title: Chilean banking crisis
events.desc: Chile's economy contracts ~14% and the state rescues the banks — the worst crisis of the Pinochet era.

events.title: Australia floats the dollar
events.desc: The Hawke–Keating government floats the AUD and deregulates finance — the start of decades of uninterrupted growth.

events.title: Plaza Accord
events.desc: G5 nations agree to weaken the dollar; the surging yen inflates the asset bubble that bursts in 1991.

events.title: Spain joins the EEC
events.desc: European Community membership anchors Spanish democracy and powers a two-decade convergence boom.

events.title: Nigeria adopts structural adjustment
events.desc: The IMF-backed SAP devalues the naira and deregulates trade after the oil-price collapse.

events.title: Đổi Mới reforms begin
events.desc: Vietnam's 'renovation' introduces market mechanisms — the launchpad for one of Asia's fastest growth stories.

events.title: Microsoft IPO
events.desc: Microsoft goes public at $21/share; Gates, 30, becomes the youngest self-made billionaire of his era a year later.

events.title: Canada–US Free Trade Agreement
events.desc: The FTA (signed 1988, in force 1989) integrates the Canadian and US economies and paves the way to NAFTA.

events.title: Arnault wins control of LVMH
events.desc: Bernard Arnault prevails in the battle for LVMH and builds it into the world's largest luxury group.

events.title: German reunification
events.desc: FRG and GDR unite; trillion-euro transfers between west and east reshape German income data for years.

events.title: Balcerowicz shock therapy
events.desc: Poland's bold stabilization plan ends hyperinflation and becomes the template for post-communist transitions.

events.title: Slim acquires Telmex
events.desc: Carlos Slim's consortium wins Mexico's Telmex privatization — the cornerstone of his telecom empire.

events.title: Japan's asset bubble bursts
events.desc: Stock and land prices collapse after the BOJ tightens; the 'Lost Decades' of stagnation begin.

events.title: India liberalizes its economy
events.desc: A balance-of-payments crisis forces the Rao–Singh reforms: licenses scrapped, trade opened — growth accelerates durably.

events.title: Convertibility plan pegs the peso
events.desc: The peso is pegged 1:1 to the dollar, killing hyperinflation — until the rigid peg snaps in 2001.

events.title: Lira crashes out of the ERM
events.desc: Sterling and the lira are forced out of the European exchange-rate mechanism on Black Wednesday.

events.title: Swedish banking crisis
events.desc: A credit bubble bursts; Sweden nationalizes banks — later textbooked as a model crisis resolution.

events.title: Russia launches shock therapy
events.desc: Price liberalization under Gaidar opens the post-Soviet economy; output collapses before recovery.

events.title: Deng's Southern Tour revives reform
events.desc: The retired Deng Xiaoping tours Shenzhen and urges faster market reforms; investment and the coastal boom reignite.

events.title: NVIDIA founded
events.desc: Jensen Huang co-founds NVIDIA — three decades later its GPUs power the AI boom and make him one of the richest people alive.

events.title: Plano Real ends hyperinflation
events.desc: Brazil's new currency, the real, breaks inflation running above 2,000% a year and stabilizes the economy.

events.title: NAFTA takes effect
events.desc: The North American Free Trade Agreement binds Mexico, the US and Canada into one trade bloc.

events.title: End of apartheid — rejoining the world
events.desc: Democratic elections end sanctions and isolation; South Africa reintegrates into the world economy.

events.title: Amazon founded
events.desc: Jeff Bezos quits D.E. Shaw and starts Amazon as an online bookstore from a Seattle garage.

events.title: Tequila crisis
events.desc: A peso devaluation spirals into a confidence crisis; a $50B US-led rescue stops the contagion.

events.title: Asian financial crisis hits Korea
events.desc: Capital flight forces South Korea into a $58B IMF package — then the largest ever — followed by rapid reform and recovery.

events.title: Baht devalued — Asian crisis starts here
events.desc: Thailand floats the baht under speculative attack; the collapse ripples across Asia within weeks.

events.title: Asian crisis topples the rupiah
events.desc: The rupiah loses ~80% of its value; Indonesia takes an IMF package and Suharto falls within a year.

events.title: Kyoto Protocol adopted
events.desc: Industrialized nations agree the first binding CO2 reduction targets in Kyoto.

events.title: Amazon goes public
events.desc: Amazon lists at $18/share — three years after its founding in a garage.

events.title: Russia defaults on its debt
events.desc: Moscow defaults on domestic bonds and devalues the ruble; LTCM collapses in the global fallout.

events.title: Google founded
events.desc: Larry Page and Sergey Brin incorporate Google in a Menlo Park garage.

events.title: Colombia's first recession in 60 years
events.desc: Colombia enters its worst downturn on record and floats the peso under an IMF program.

events.title: Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance, founded
events.desc: Gavi pools donors to fund immunization in poor countries — hundreds of millions of children vaccinated since.

events.title: China joins the WTO
events.desc: Accession to the World Trade Organization supercharges export-led growth; China becomes the workshop of the world.

events.title: Argentina defaults and devalues
events.desc: The corralito, a $95B default and the end of the dollar peg — the worst crisis in Argentine history.

events.title: Turkey's banking crisis
events.desc: A liquidity panic collapses the lira; an IMF-backed program ("Strong Economy") restructures Turkish banking.

events.title: Crédit Agricole lists in Paris
events.desc: The French mutual banking giant goes public, raising capital for its expansion into investment banking.

events.title: Inditex IPO
events.desc: Ortega takes Inditex (Zara) public; it becomes the world's largest fashion retailer.

events.title: SMFG is formed
events.desc: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation emerges from the Sumitomo–Sakura merger — Japan's megabank consolidation accelerates.

events.title: Musk starts SpaceX
events.desc: Elon Musk invests ~$100M of his PayPal proceeds into SpaceX; a year later he joins Tesla as lead investor.

events.title: Poland joins the European Union
events.desc: EU accession caps fifteen years of transition and unlocks record investment and emigration-driven remittances.

events.title: HIV/AIDS peak & ART scale-up
events.desc: AIDS mortality peaks in southern Africa as the WHO "3 by 5" drive and national programs roll out antiretroviral therapy.

events.title: Google IPO
events.desc: Google goes public at $85/share via its unconventional Dutch auction, making Brin and Page billionaires.

events.title: Facebook founded in a dorm
events.desc: Mark Zuckerberg launches TheFacebook from his Harvard room; within a decade it reaches a billion users.

events.title: CCB goes public in Hong Kong
events.desc: China Construction Bank raises $9.2B — the first of China's big-four state banks to list.

events.title: MUFG is formed
events.desc: Mitsubishi Tokyo Financial Group and UFJ Holdings merge, creating one of the world's largest banks.

events.title: BoCom lists in Hong Kong
events.desc: Bank of Communications becomes the first mainland Chinese bank to list abroad.

events.title: ICBC's record-breaking IPO
events.desc: Industrial & Commercial Bank of China raises $21.9B — the largest IPO in history at the time.

events.title: Bank of China IPO
events.desc: Bank of China raises $11.2B in Hong Kong and Shanghai — part of the big-four opening to markets.

events.title: Global financial crisis
events.desc: Lehman Brothers fails; the Great Recession ripples through incomes worldwide for years.

events.title: Switzerland rescues UBS
events.desc: The Swiss state injects CHF 6B into UBS and parks $60B of toxic assets — a rare bailout in Swiss history.

events.title: JPMorgan absorbs Bear Stearns and WaMu
events.desc: In the depths of the crisis JPMorgan takes over Bear Stearns ($2/share, Fed-backed) and Washington Mutual's assets.

events.title: BofA buys Merrill Lynch
events.desc: Bank of America acquires Merrill Lynch for ~$50B at the height of the panic — later needing $45B in TARP aid.

events.title: Citigroup bailout
events.desc: Citi receives $45B in TARP capital plus guarantees on $306B of assets to avert collapse.

events.title: Fannie Mae in conservatorship
events.desc: The US government places Fannie Mae under conservatorship and backstops it with up to $100B.

events.title: Freddie Mac in conservatorship
events.desc: Freddie Mac joins Fannie Mae under government control — the mortgage giants are nationalized in all but name.

events.title: AgBank's world-record IPO
events.desc: Agricultural Bank of China raises $22.1B, overtaking ICBC as the largest IPO ever (a record it held until Alibaba 2014).

events.title: Berkshire buys BNSF Railway
events.desc: Buffett's largest acquisition ever (~$44B): an all-in bet on the US economy's freight backbone.

events.title: HSBC's $1.9B laundering settlement
events.desc: HSBC pays a record US fine for laundering drug-cartel and sanctioned-country money.

events.title: Facebook IPO
events.desc: Facebook raises $16B at a $104B valuation — the biggest tech IPO to date, despite a glitchy debut.

events.title: Dell goes private
events.desc: Michael Dell and Silver Lake take Dell private for $24.9B — the largest tech buyout then.

events.title: BNP Paribas fined $8.9B
events.desc: BNP pleads guilty to US sanctions violations — the largest penalty ever imposed on a bank at that point.

events.title: Paris Agreement on climate
events.desc: 196 parties commit in Paris to keep warming well below 2 °C and pursue 1.5 °C.

events.title: Japan Post privatization IPO
events.desc: Japan Post Holdings lists in a ¥1.4 trillion offering — the world's biggest IPO of 2015.

events.title: Brexit referendum
events.desc: The UK votes to leave the EU; the pound drops to 30-year lows overnight.

events.title: Demonetization
events.desc: India voids 86% of its cash overnight to fight black money — a massive, disruptive natural experiment.

events.title: Saudi Vision 2030
events.desc: Mohammed bin Salman's plan to wean the kingdom off oil: privatization, new sectors, social liberalization.

events.title: Egypt floats the pound under IMF deal
events.desc: The central bank floats the EGP, which halves in value, as part of a $12B IMF program.

events.title: Fake-accounts scandal
events.desc: Wells Fargo admits staff opened millions of unauthorized accounts; $185M in fines, the CEO resigns, the brand is damaged for years.

events.title: PSBC goes public
events.desc: Postal Savings Bank of China raises $7.4B in Hong Kong — the world's largest IPO of 2016.

events.title: Reliance Jio launches
events.desc: Mukesh Ambani's Jio floods India with free 4G data, upending the telecom market and re-rating Reliance.

events.title: Turkish currency crisis
events.desc: The lira plunges amid unorthodox rate policy and a US dispute; inflation hits 25%.

events.title: Aramco IPO — the largest ever
events.desc: Saudi Aramco lists ~1.5% at a $1.7T valuation, raising $25.6B — the biggest IPO in history.

events.title: COVID-19 pandemic
events.desc: A novel coronavirus in Wuhan becomes a global pandemic; life expectancy drops in most countries.

events.title: COVAX launches
events.desc: WHO, Gavi and CEPI launch COVAX to pool COVID-19 vaccine procurement for equitable access.

events.title: LVMH buys Tiffany & Co.
events.desc: LVMH completes the $15.8B Tiffany acquisition — the largest deal in luxury history.

events.title: Musk buys Twitter
events.desc: After a months-long legal fight, Musk closes the $44B Twitter takeover and rebrands it X.
